steps.title: Spot the narrator's mental state
steps.content: The strongest signal: “We no longer speak, think nor live; we float along through space in delicious inertia.” The passage explicitly states that the narrator's normal consciousness has completely ceased—all attention is now focused on the floating sensation.

steps.title: Apply the mental state to the choices
steps.content: Choice B is correct because when thinking and speaking have ceased and all attention rests on the floating experience, that defines complete absorption in the perspective change. The other choices describe isolation despite proximity (A), distress from altitude (C), or external observation of companions' enthusiasm (D), none capturing the passage's focus on ceased normal thought and inner focus. That matches choice B — B is correct.
